_comment: ADOPT: the control state of record (replaces any hand-graded scorecard). Five states: absent | defined | mechanically-validated | platform-enforced | organisationally-enforced — each state demands receipts (scripts/control-catalog-check.mjs fails the build on an overstated entry). As shipped, the bundle's gates grade mechanically-validated; platform-enforced is YOURS to claim once branch protection is active with a negative bypass test recorded in activation_evidence. Execution fields (read by core/gate-runner.mjs): `lane` pr|release|scheduled, `always` = tamper-detection core that never skips in its lane, `paths` = prefixes that implicate the control on a diff (no paths = runs by default), `execute:false` = library/composed control enforced via another gate. Skipping is always RECORDED, never silent.

controls.objective: Four-eyes merge: the agent proposes, a human disposes; no self-merge
controls.note: Becomes platform-enforced only when branch protection is active — record the rejected direct-push probe as activation_evidence

controls.objective: Immutable control plane: guardrails cannot be changed without Code Owner review; placeholder owners rejected
The control catalog itself cannot overstate a control's state
Approvals resolve to human registry identities; agents approve nothing; second-line ∩ builders = ∅
Every governed change is classified by a human with authority, plan-reconciled at execution, and advances states only with receipts
Test integrity: a red bar goes green by fixing the code, never by weakening the test
controls.execute_note: needs --base <merge-base>; CI invokes it directly with the PR base

controls.objective: No secret-shaped content in the tree OR the reachable git history
Waist gate: no feature enters delivery without a gate-green discovery hand-off
Discovery runs are evidence-traced, register-mapped, brand-real and stakeholder-tested (D1–D9)
controls.execute_note: runs per discovery run directory; CI iterates discovery/runs/*

controls.objective: Every data category declares classification, lawful basis, bounded retention, erasure disposition, residency
Run→Discovery: operational signals are triaged, owned, traceable — and MANDATORY once anything is in production
SAST output is real SARIF from a named tool and clears the severity policy
Every model role is pinned, tiered, evaluated against its pin with a sealed report, independently validated, and (at high tier) declares runtime monitoring, suspension thresholds and a fallback
PA1/PA2: a product cannot enter Develop or reach launch without the compiled sections and resolvable control-function approvals
Architecture assurance A1–A5 with threat→control→test traceability; material open findings block delivery
A change's risk classification compiles its required gates, approvers and evidence; higher tiers only add (monotonic)
Release evidence is a sealed, commit-bound, semantically-verified hash chain (a symbolic release_commit is rejected)

The release binds to an immutable artifact digest: a real source commit, a digest-pinned artifact and the trusted builder that produced it; symbolic subjects and mutable (tag-pinned) uris are rejected
controls.note: The gate proves the subject is coherent and internally consistent (mechanically-validated); that the artifact was actually built by that builder from that commit is the platform's signed provenance, cross-checked by release-attestation-check and observed live in WS2. Mandatory-when-compiled: a null artifact fails once a governed change is in production

controls.objective: Source, artifact, product evaluations and the sealed anchor all name ONE immutable artifact digest — dissolving the commit self-reference (F1): evidence cannot be reused for another build, and a commit-only eval that predates the artifact cannot pass
controls.note: Cross-binding verifier (mechanically-validated): it proves the RECORDS agree on the digest and the anchor is authentically signed. Live observation of the registry and the running deployment is the platform's (WS2). Mandatory-when-compiled: a production release with no artifact-bound subject fails

controls.objective: The release carries a real SBOM, an in-policy dependency audit, and builder-bound provenance
R1–R6 operational readiness with freshness windows: an expired DR exercise, undrilled rollback or untested kill-switch blocks production
Production authorization is compound: PA2 + R-gate-green readiness + second-line hold released + anchored, issuer-verified evidence at high tiers
The second line can hold any release; a missing hold means HELD (fail closed); builders and agents cannot release it
An evidence anchor counts only when signed by a registry issuer and cryptographically verified (ed25519 in-process; platform mechanisms report UNVERIFIED-HERE until wired)
controls.execute_note: library — enforced via change-envelope-check at production states

controls.objective: Risk-proportionate execution: lanes + diff scoping from this catalog, with every skip recorded, never silent
controls.execute_note: the runner itself — CI invokes it per lane

controls.objective: PII-shaped literals are blocked at write time — synthetic data only, everywhere
The API contract cannot change inside a feature branch — spec-only PRs
Test-disabling edits are blocked at write time (defence in depth with Q1B)
Least-privilege agent identity, vaulted secrets, no shared credentials
Environment promotion with rehearsed rollback and change-ticket linkage
A named accountable executive with cease-use authority over the harness
Residency-controlled model traffic, pre-egress DLP, attested execution
An organisationally-separate model-risk function owning validation and runtime monitoring
An independent second-line challenge FUNCTION (people, mandate, veto)
controls.note: The release-hold MECHANISM ships (RELEASE-HOLD); the organisationally-separate function operating it is the adopter's — organisationally-enforced is theirs to claim. See docs/governance/runbooks/independent-assurance-runbook.md

controls.objective: Internal-audit read access and independent re-performance of the evidence assessment
controls.note: Requires an adopter's audit function and external WORM store — see docs/governance/runbooks/independent-assurance-runbook.md

controls.objective: A replayable, append-only hash chain of agent decisions and tool use — contiguous, tamper-evident, reconstructable
controls.note: Integrity ships (mechanically-validated); CAPTURE — writing entries as the agent works — is the adopter's harness wiring, so this is not yet platform-enforced

controls.objective: A signed continuous-assurance cycle record (six lifecycle steps) with an unresolved-findings register, confirmed by a second-line human

A control graduates to platform-enforced only on a fresh, independently-signed observation that the live platform mechanism is active AND refused a negative bypass test; the observer is outside the agent's write authority; a platform-enforced catalog claim without such a receipt fails
controls.note: The gate VERIFIES observations (mechanically-validated): the observation is signed, independent, bypass-tested and fresh. Producing the observation — `loom activate --platform github` querying the live platform with read-only credentials — is the adopter's; the bundle ships ZERO platform-enforced controls, so graduation passes trivially here and the negative test proves an overstated claim fails

controls.objective: The live control-plane configuration is continuously reconciled against the approved baseline; a removed required check, weakened CODEOWNERS, re-enabled force-push, widened workflow token, swapped OIDC issuer, or an AI identity added to an approval group is drift that fails the gate and suspends routine auto-merge
controls.note: The gate reconciles records (mechanically-validated): baseline vs observed vs registry. The CURRENT observation is produced adopter-side by `loom activate` (live platform read). On drift, the routine-change gate fails closed via routine-envelope.suspended, and the WS2.3 controller — gating auto-merge on this gate — does not merge

controls.objective: Auto-merge is enabled by a trusted bot identity DISJOINT from the coding agent, only for a qualifying routine PR with config-reconciliation green, and every decision is written to the evidence record
controls.note: The reference controller workflow ships (routine-controller.yml): it runs routine-qualified + config-reconciliation under a separated bot token and enables auto-merge only on both green. Running it with a real, separated bot identity and a merge-queue ruleset is the adopter's — platform-enforced is theirs to claim once the separated identity and ruleset are active and a bypass is recorded

controls.objective: Enterprise adapters map external systems to catalog controls via signed evidence envelopes, keeping the core vendor-neutral

Graduated autonomy: a routine change auto-merges only inside a second-line-owned, expiring envelope; an absolute floor keeps the control plane, contract, auth and migrations out of the lane; anything else re-evaluates to human merge
controls.note: The gate validates that a claimed routine change fits the envelope (mechanically-validated). Platform-enforced is the adopter's: a merge-queue/ruleset that auto-merges only with this gate among the passing required checks, with the out-of-envelope rejection recorded as activation_evidence

controls.objective: Evals-as-product-management: a released product links its discovery hand-off, scores every D1 success measure green, and carries a fresh sealed eval run against the shipping commit; a regression or a stale eval blocks the release
controls.note: The gate enforces a fresh, discovery-linked, sealed eval (mechanically-validated); the eval RIG that scores each measure is the adopter's to build

controls.objective: Institutional conformance: a governed change in a BrainKit-enabled repository binds to an approved, sealed, owner-accountable, source-grounded Institutional BrainKit; a draft/expired/retired or tampered BrainKit, an unresolved owner, a stale D7 compatibility projection, or a plan pinning the wrong BrainKit digest blocks the change. Institutionally conformant, not regulatorily compliant
controls.note: Mandatory-when-compiled: enforced when a change's compiled plan carries the brainkit-conformance family (an institution profile is in the envelope); a dormant, adopt-pending BrainKit that no change pins is skipped. The BrainKit CONTENT is the institution's to author and approve — the gate enforces its integrity, not its correctness

controls.objective: Guardrail coverage is explicit and runtime-neutral: the policy names only protections that exist, marks every uncovered runtime honestly, and acknowledges any blocking guardrail enforced nowhere — the Loom never implies equivalent enforcement where a runtime lacks the hook
controls.note: The gate proves the policy is honest and the capability matrix (guardrails/README.md, doc-integrity-gated) tracks it. The local hooks are Claude Code-specific; the CI gates are the enforcement of record where a runtime lacks a hook. Hostile scenarios (PII literal, test weakening) are driven through the real adapters in the test

controls.objective: Adoption is a five-stage state machine (installed → configured → validated → platform → organisation) projected from the control catalog; a signed adoption report cannot be produced while any mandatory item is adopt-pending, and an agent cannot certify its own adoption
controls.execute_note: adopter tool (loom status / attest-adoption), run on demand — not a per-PR merge gate (a reference repo is intentionally adopt-pending). The gate ships mechanically-validated; producing the signed report is the adopter's
controls.note: loom status projects the stage matrix + the unresolved-marker inventory; attest-adoption refuses to sign while adopt-pending (closes F7). The signature is adopter-side

controls.objective: Generated docs (copy table, scorecard) cannot drift from their source of truth — drift fails the build
The compiler's per-change requirements are aggregated as one source of truth the runner and gates read
controls.execute_note: library — enforced via the gate runner and the mandatory-when-compiled gates

controls.objective: The bank-grade scorecard is a projection of the control catalog, not hand-maintained prose
controls.execute_note: library — enforced via the doc-integrity gate

controls.objective: External WORM + RFC-3161 timestamped evidence retention, independently examinable
controls.note: The evidence-seal gate makes the bundle tamper-evident and anchor-checkable; the immutable store + timestamping authority are the adopter's (independent-assurance-runbook.md)

controls.objective: Dynamic application security testing and penetration testing
controls.note: Add as gates / scheduled assurance; the bundle stops at SAST/SCA output validation (security-testing-and-resilience-runbook.md)

controls.objective: A supervised production pilot on real, bounded, reversible scope
controls.note: The bundle ships the pilot playbook + adversarial checklist (runbooks/pilot-playbook.md); running it is the adopter's

controls.objective: A live regulator examination of the method in production
controls.note: The proof the method has not yet earned — adoption is not regulatory certification

controls.objective: The real-PII control surface: KMS, field encryption, tokenization, access logging
controls.note: The harness is synthetic-only by design; the real-data surface is the adopter's (data-protection-runbook.md)
